hypothesis: Incorporating synthetic MOF-specific pre-training data generated by chemically-constrained large language models—combining novel hypothetical MOFid strings with their computationally predicted properties—will outperform pre-training on static corpora alone (PMC articles and PubChem compounds) in low-data regimes (SFT training sets below 5K samples), because dynamically generated training examples can systematically cover underrepresented regions of the MOF chemical space that are absent from existing databases like CoRE MOF 2019, QMOF, and hMOF, thereby improving generalization on property prediction tasks such as band gap estimation and gas adsorption by at least 15% in MAE relative to the best static pre-training configuration identified in this study.
